concerns.text: T017 calculates FID against a 'held-out RealEstate10K reference set' (absolute quality), violating SC-002 which mandates measuring FID of the sparse method's output *against the dense baseline* (relative trade-off). This semantic weakening changes the scientific metric from a comparative study to an absolute quality check.

T017 computes WorldScore for the dense baseline but defines the sparse metric as 'Sparse-Consistency Score' (re-projection error). SC-001 and FR-005 require a direct comparison of 'topological fidelity' (WorldScore) between dense and sparse methods. Using different metrics for the ANOVA input violates the requirement to compare the same property across methods.

T009 enforces 'exactly 50 sequences per stratum' with a 'fail fast' error if fewer are found. FR-001 requires 'n≥50' to ensure statistical power. The task's rigid equality constraint is a semantic weakening that risks pipeline failure on valid datasets with >50 sequences per stratum, contradicting the spec's flexibility.

T008b fetches/validates dense baseline data and T016 loads it from a generated file. The Spec's 'Assumptions' state the dense baseline is 'provided as a pre-computed baseline' (external input). The tasks treat it as an internal pipeline artifact to be generated/fetched dynamically, violating the constitutional principle of data source integrity and the spec's assumption of external provision.

T021 generates a report summarizing 'Inference time reduction' but lacks a verification step to calculate the percentage difference and compare it against the SC-003 threshold (≥40%). The task delivers a summary without the mandatory pass/fail verification of the success criterion, weakening the acceptance criteria.
